title: anthoxanthum brunonis
intro: Anthoxanthum brunonis, also known as Brunon's oat grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. The health benefits of Anthoxanthum brunonis include its ability to calm digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its leaves and roots, and are available in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions.
